_comment: Captured live 2026-07-10 from trello.com/c/TcE3br7d, an open card modal with one
checklist (9 items, 89%). browser.extract_elements {item_selector:"[data-testid]"}
scoped to the card-back-name ancestor. READ-ONLY capture; no mutation.
WHY THIS FILE EXISTS: three static-lint hypotheses for catching dead testids were
each refuted by a positive control (investigations/trello-checklist-operations-
unreliable.md). A selector string cannot say whether a testid exists, nor whether
one testid WRAPS another -- containment lives only in the rendered DOM. This is that
DOM, frozen. With it, 'does this testid exist' is a lookup instead of a guess.
CAUTION, learned the hard way: a scoped enumeration lists DESCENDANTS of its scope.
'Absent from a scoped query' NEVER means 'does not exist'. checklist-container was
declared dead on exactly that error -- it is the PARENT of checklist-section.
⚠️ SECOND CAUTION, learned 2026-07-10 by generalising from this very file:
This card had ONE settled checklist, so checklist-container and checklist-section
had identical text and I inferred they wrap one-to-one. THEY DO NOT.
Trello renders ONE checklist-container PER CHECKLIST. Only a HYDRATED checklist
additionally gets an inner checklist-section / checklist-title. A freshly created
one has a container and nothing else.
So a DOM fixture answers 'does this testid exist' -- in the state captured. It cannot
answer 'does it exist in the state my workflow will encounter', nor 'how many are
there when N objects exist'. Capture the states the workflow traverses. n=1 is not
an ontology.
⚠️ THIRD CAUTION, 2026-07-10, caught by a control before it cost anything:
DO NOT write an audit rule of the form `testid not in fixture.testids => dead`.
This census is SCOPED to 'ancestor of [data-testid=card-back-name]', and a scoped
enumeration lists DESCENDANTS -- so the scope ANCHOR ITSELF is absent from the list.
`card-back-name` is not in `testids`, yet it exists (live count: 1) and
checklist_item.complete's assertCardModal binds it successfully on every run.
That rule would have flagged a healthy step and told me to delete it. False positives
on a detector that prompts edits cost more than false negatives (reliable-measurement
§5.5): a missed finding leaves the code as it was; a false one makes you break working
code, confidently.
THE SOUND PREDICATE is membership in `known_dead` -- a CURATED set, where every entry
names the live instruments that corroborated its absence. Absence from `testids` is
evidence of nothing. Adding an entry requires two independent live probes.

containment._comment: Inferred from identical text content: a parent whose text equals its child's wraps it with nothing between. This is the relationship no CSS selector can express.

text_samples.checklist-container: StepsHide checked itemsDelete89%L1: finish trello.card.delete fix...

known_dead._comment: Testids referenced by maps but ABSENT from this capture. Verified absent, not merely out-of-scope: this census was scoped to the whole card modal.
known_dead.checklist-progress-bar-percentage: 0 matches. checklist.read binds it FIRST, with checklist-container (its ANCESTOR) as fallback -- the fallback always wins, so the read silently returns the wrong element. This is #171.
known_dead.check-item-name-input: absent while no item composer is open; capture again with the composer open before calling it dead
known_dead.checklist-add-button: absent while no popover is open; NOT dead -- lives in the add-checklist popover
known_dead.checklist-button: absent from the modal; lives in the card HEADER, outside this scope
known_dead.card-back-content: 0 matches. Corroborated live 2026-07-10 by TWO independent instruments: viewport.scroll{scope} -> target_not_found, and debug.run_javascript querySelectorAll -> 0. Not state-dependent: it purports to be the card-body SCROLL CONTAINER, which would exist in every state if it existed at all. checklist_item.complete scrolled it behind on_error:'continue', so the step failed silently on EVERY run and nothing ever scrolled. The real container is an unlabelled <main> with a hashed class -- never bind it; use locator.element_info, which auto-scrolls its match. This is #208.

state_caveats.captured_state: one settled checklist ('Steps', 8/9), no popover open, no item composer open
state_caveats.checklist-container: ONE PER CHECKLIST. Present even for a brand-new checklist. Disambiguate with text_contains.
state_caveats.checklist-section: only on a HYDRATED checklist. Absent immediately after create.
state_caveats.checklist-title: only on a HYDRATED checklist. Absent immediately after create.
state_caveats.checklist-delete-button: only on a HYDRATED checklist. The single match on a mixed card belongs to the OLD one -- clicking it destroys the wrong checklist.
state_caveats.add-checklist popover: carries NO data-testid. Title field is id='id-checklist'; the copy-from select is id='copy-items-from-select'.
